title: Fix TextEdit IME error on mouse over
body: - Fixes hovering over a line with IME text causing errors `ERROR: scene/gui/text_edit.cpp:6402 - Condition "p_column > text[p_line].length()" is true. Returning: 0` - Fixes search highlight being offset when there is IME text before it - Fixes minimap not being correct when using IME (wrapped lines were messed up) - Fixes (maybe) https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/110316 - I'm not sure if this is the same issue or not - Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110787 since that fixes the mouse over issue - But I don't think it actually fixes () https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/110138 since that describes Chinese IME specific issues and does not mention mouse movement. Fixed `get_line_wrap_index_at_column` not accepting column with IME. `get_line_wrapped_text` used `text.get_line_wrap_ranges` which uses `data_buf` for the line wraps, which is uses IME text. So it was using the non-IME text string with IME line wraps, which was causing the minimap and search highlight issues. Also made `get_line_wrap_index_at_column` use the `get_line_wrap_ranges` directly, it doesn't need to get the text first. There are lots more issues with IME here, anything using the TextServer or `data_buf` is using the text with the IME, but some functions use those together with things that don't use the IME text, like the carets, selections, and text strings. Such as `get_rect_at_line_column`, `get_word`, most places that use `get_line_column_at_pos`, and more. Search highlight issue (with an open find panel):
author: kitbdev

linked_issues.title: [4.5] Script Editor popup error when using <Microsoft Input Method>
IME preedit apostrophe (U+0027) leaks into committed text → multiple regressions in editor since 4.3/4.4 (TextEdit caret error; Find-in-Files desync; apostrophes between letters)

issue_comments.body: Sorry I test the pr but it still causes errors on Windows 11 (Microsoft Pinyin IME) `ERROR: scene\gui\text_edit.cpp:4971 - Index p_column = 4 is out of bounds (text_line.size() + 1 = 4). when hovering a line with IME text ` https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/33fe0f23-4a9e-414e-8c8d-17b75d4cb63f the case③ of #110138

more details base on testing for the pr :

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/29262d37-7de2-4588-9d58-02239001c043

it shows case①  case②  case③ for #110138  and #103180 and maybe the same isuue for #81990

It looks that issue happens in `TextEdit::get_word` and it occurs with other language IMEs as well when it tries to lookup the word for the symbol tooltip. To reproduce with other languages, have the IME at multiple carets so it doesn't block the text and hover or ctrl hover over one of them.
It is more easily reproducible with the Chinese IME because it doesn't block the current line from receiving mouse input.

Fixed it in a similar way, `get_word` now uses IME text consistently and accepts column with IME.
Hi @bruvzg, just wondering whether this is still planned to be merged for 4.6. Thanks!

review_comments.body: `get_line_wrap_ranges` always uses non-ime text buffer, so mixing it with `text.get_text_with_ime` for column range won't fully work. There are some other places it is mixed as well.

`get_line_wrap_ranges` uses `data_buf`, which is does use IME.
There are some other mixed up places in TextEdit, but I don't think this is one of them and those would take more effort to fix.
Indeed, I mixed it up, there's only one `data_buf`, so it should be fine.

label_notes: TextEdit IME triple-bug fix (mouse-hover error, search highlight offset, minimap). kitbdev (CONTRIBUTOR, prior=172) iterated with reviewer on IME text buffer mixing semantics.
